title: Concurrency / Recovery / Schema Migration Governance
intent: Add dynamic Story/file/contract conflict checks, recovery policy, and schema migration policy without introducing stale long-lived lock files.
scope_delta: add dynamic file and contract conflict checks
standardize CR relationship fields for dependencies, recommendations, references, blockers, supersession, enablement, weak relations, and derivation
non_goals: no full migration implementation for every legacy project
no mandatory rollback plan for docs-lite and micro changes
expected_outputs: CR relationship schema or template fields
CR relationship validation for depends_on, recommended_after, references, blocks, blocked_by, supersedes, superseded_by, enables, related_to, and derived_from
CR conflict validation remains based on conflict_keys, impact_surface, affected docs/features/modules, authz policy refs, and risk refs

depends_on.meaning: Hard dependency; prerequisite CR must be closed, ready, or otherwise explicitly satisfied before this CR starts.

recommended_after.meaning: Recommended order; parallel execution is allowed only with an explicit rationale.

references.meaning: Background, policy, upstream decision, or related historical context reference.

blocks.meaning: This CR blocks another CR, gate, capability, release, or follow-up item.

blocked_by.meaning: This CR is blocked by another CR, gate, risk, missing authorization, or unresolved decision.

supersedes.meaning: This CR replaces an older CR or candidate item.

superseded_by.meaning: This CR has been replaced by another CR and should not continue.

enables.meaning: This CR unlocks a later CR, capability, profile, or workflow path after completion.

derived_from.meaning: This CR was derived from another CR, CP decision, follow-up candidate, spike candidate, issue, or user request.

blocks: quant-lab code migration and multi-story module split
derived_from: post-MF-017 engineering delivery governance gap analysis

quant_lab_relevance: Required before quant-lab code migration, module split, package identity migration, and multi-story refactor execution.
